commit.message:
perf: optimize memory storage query() and count() for find-by-query (#8058)

* Initial plan

* perf: optimize memory storage query() and count() methods

- query(): Add fast path when selector is satisfied by index - use pre-allocated
  array with indexed assignment instead of per-doc push with matcher checks.
  Skip unnecessary rows.slice() when no skip/limit is applied.
- count(): Compute count directly from index range bounds when selector is
  satisfied by index, avoiding full document array allocation.
- Clean up redundant `upperBound = upperBound` assignment.

Performance results (10 runs, 3000 docs):
  find-by-query:          2.731ms -> 2.427ms (11% faster)
  find-by-query-parallel: 3.507ms -> 3.376ms (4% faster)
  4x-count:               1.211ms -> 0.303ms (75% faster)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use newRxError instead of throw new Error in count() method

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(recall): add WITH clause between YIELD and WHERE for KuzuDB compatibility

KuzuDB's CALL...YIELD syntax does not support a WHERE clause directly
after YIELD. A WITH clause must be inserted between YIELD and WHERE.

Fixes scoped recall queries for both vector (QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX) and
full-text (QUERY_FTS_INDEX) search paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: agents now comment/react/post with real diversity

- Pre-assign action type in scheduler (20% post, 50% comment, 20% react) — LLM provides content only
- Feed diversity: mix newest 15 + 30 random posts so agents see different topics
- Action normalization: reply/respond/post_comment all map correctly
- Turn prompt: tells agent their assigned action, injects target post for comments/reacts
- Fallback safeContent prevents empty-content errors
- UI feed auto-refreshes every 5s
- Fixed react handler: always picks random emoji if no valid emoji provided

Results: 30 comments + 14 reacts + 15 posts per 60s, zero errors, diverse topics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: strip id: prefix from LLM target_post_id, faster wake intervals, more parallel agents

- Strip 'id:' prefix agents copy verbatim from feed into target_post_id
- Wake interval: 120-900s → 15-90s (10x faster)

- Prompt: strongly prefer comment/react (60%) when feed has posts

feat(timeout): add deadline and timeout utilities (#498)

New package for running functions with time limits:
- Do[T]: run function with timeout, return result or timeout error
- DoSimple: simplified Do for functions returning only error
- After[T]: run function async, receive result on channel
- Race[T]: run multiple functions, return first result

All functions are context-aware and cancel child contexts on completion.
Includes doc.go, 15+ unit tests, 2 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve type definitions: fix bug, document password typing, narrow types (#8056)

* Initial plan

* fix: improve type definitions across codebase

- Fix undefined `context` variable bug in sqlite-storage-instance.ts findDocumentsById
- Narrow `password` field from `string | any` (effectively `any`) to `string` in RxDatabaseCreator, RxStorageInstanceCreationParams, RxErrorParameters, and RxDatabaseBase
- Replace `type: 'object' | string` with `type: 'object'` in RxJsonSchema (old TypeScript workaround no longer needed)
- Address RxDumpCollectionAsAny TODO: change from `{ [P in keyof T]: any }` to `T` (preserves original property types)
- Improve `_saveData` oldData parameter from `any` to `RxDocumentData<RxDocType>`
- Remove outdated comments about encrypted properties being typed as any

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert password type narrowing and add comment explaining why it is typed as any

Different encryption plugins may use passwords that are not strings,
so the password field must remain typed as `string | any`.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert RxJsonSchema.type narrowing to fix CI type errors

The 'object' | string type is still needed because TypeScript infers
inline object literals' type field as string, not the literal 'object'.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: improve BT scan UX — Pair & Add, troubleshooting tips, default-agent

- Add 'Pair & Add' button in scan results: pair + trust + add to config
  in one click with progress feedback and SSP auto-confirmation
- New /api/bt/pair_new endpoint for standalone pairing by MAC without
  requiring an existing client/config entry
- Show troubleshooting tips when scan finds no devices (pairing mode,
  proximity, unpair from other sources)
- Add 'default-agent' to scan flow for better device discovery
- Increase scan duration from 10s to 15s for slower-advertising devices
- Fix group badge tooltip showing duplicate players: filter external
  members by name match (bridge and MA use different player ID formats)

Feature/web UI (#15)

* feat: Flask web UI for repo scanning with SSE streaming and Mermaid rendering

- Add web/app.py: Flask server with POST /scan endpoint that streams
  pipeline output via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- Add web/templates/index.html: dark-themed UI with real-time log output
  and Mermaid.js diagram rendering
- Add Flask>=3.0.0 to requirements.txt

The UI allows specifying a server-side repo path, runs the offline
Phase 1-3 pipeline (run_pipeline.py) via subprocess, streams stdout/stderr
line-by-line to the browser, and renders the generated architecture
Mermaid diagrams once the scan completes.

SSE event types: log | diagrams | error | done

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: replace repo path text input with prepopulated dropdown

Reads Intake/ReposToScan.txt and resolves each repo name against
common search roots (repo sibling dirs, ~/code, ~/repos, ~/projects, ~/).
Found repos appear as selectable options showing name + resolved path;
unfound repos are shown as disabled/greyed-out entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: auto-fill scan name from selected repo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct run_pipeline.py paths for Scripts/Utils/ location

SCRIPTS was resolving to Scripts/Utils/ (the script's own dir) instead
of Scripts/, causing ModuleNotFoundError for db_helpers and broken
subprocess paths for triage_experiment.py, discover_code_context.py,
render_finding.py and generate_diagram.py.

- Walk up one extra level: SCRIPTS = parent.parent → Scripts/
- Add Scripts/Persist and Scripts/Utils to sys.path for imports
- Replace bare SCRIPTS / 'script.py' with explicit path constants
  pointing to the correct subdirectory for each script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: replace SVG icon with 🦕 emoji and centre header

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resilient imports in triage_experiment.py when running from Scripts/Experiments

- Add Scripts/Utils and other script dirs to sys.path to make imports work
  when the script is executed directly
- Provide fallback shims when learning_db or cozo_helpers are unavailable
  so experiments can still be created on filesystem without external deps

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add Web UI usage and security warning to README

- Document how to run web/app.py and the local-only intent
- Warn that the web UI executes the pipeline on the server filesystem and
  can be abused if exposed; provide mitigation recommendations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: ensure log output shows scrollbars (max-height, overflow, custom scrollbar styling)

* fix: output_paths REPO_ROOT calculation (was pointing to Scripts/)

Use parents[2] so REPO_ROOT resolves to the repository root when file
is located at Scripts/Utils/output_paths.py. This prevents duplicate
'Scripts/Scripts' paths when other scripts construct paths from REPO_ROOT.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Allow faulty-thingy route from tooling

The faulty-thingy HTTPRoute was created in the shared tooling namespace, but the dedicated Gateway listener only allowed routes from namespaces labeled app=faulty-thingy. Because the tooling namespace does not carry that label, kgateway rejected the route with NoMatchingParent, leaving the listener with zero attached routes and causing requests for faulty-thingy.kim.tec.br to fall through to the default 404 response.

Switch the https-faulty-thingy listener to allowedRoutes.namespaces.from=All so the HTTPRoute in tooling can attach successfully, matching the shared-namespace pattern already used by other tooling applications in this repository.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix streaming content not visible until message completes

When a response completes quickly, CancelPending() on the
StreamingTextAccumulator kills the throttled UI flush before it
ever runs. The completion handler then sees streamingMsg as null
and renders the entire message at once — no streaming visible.

Fix: call FlushAssistantDelta()/FlushReasoningDelta() at the start
of each completion handler so any buffered deltas are applied before
the final content is set. This ensures the streaming message is
created and added to the UI even when completion arrives before the
throttle interval elapses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace Neo4j with KuzuDB (embedded, serverless)

KuzuDB is a fully embedded graph database — no server, no credentials,
no Docker. The database lives at ~/.local/share/neo-memory/db by
default (override with KUZU_DB_PATH).

Changes:
- Remove neo4j-driver, install kuzu@0.11.3
- config.ts: replace NEO4J_* vars with KUZU_DB_PATH
- db.ts: rewrite with kuzu Database + Connection; bootstrapSchema
  creates node/rel tables (IF NOT EXISTS) and loads fts + vector
  extensions (pre-bundled in v0.11.3)
- All tools: replace driver.session() / session.run() with runQuery()
  helper that uses prepare+execute for parameterised queries
- recall.ts: QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX (cosine HNSW) + QUERY_FTS_INDEX (BM25)
- explore.ts: two-query approach — reachable nodes then edges between
  them — avoids complex path property extraction
- index.ts: simplified startup, no connection test session
- README.md: remove Neo4j server requirement, document KUZU_DB_PATH

All smoke tests pass: schema bootstrap, INSERT, FTS search, MERGE,
RELATES_TO create, variable-length traversal, DETACH DELETE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: neo-memory-mcp

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use player_id instead of player_name for MA group lookup

get_ma_group_for_player() is keyed by player_id (UUID), but was called
with player_name (human-readable string), causing the lookup to always
return None. This made ma_group_play() never execute, so play after
pause only resumed one subprocess via fallback instead of the whole
MA syncgroup.

commit.message:
feat: Dedicated subagent UX with standalone cards and nested tool activity

Replace flat tool-call rendering of subagents with dedicated transcript
items that show each subagent as its own StrataThink card at the turn
level. Child tools are nested under their parent subagent card.

Key changes:
- Add ParentToolCallId to ChatMessage for parent-child tool hierarchy
- New SubagentToolCallItem (extends TranscriptItem) with Activities,
  progress tracking, duration, status, and agent metadata
- TranscriptBuilder routes agent:* / task tools into ProcessSubagentToolMessage
  which creates standalone turn-level subagent cards
- FindOwningSubagent walks the parent chain to nest child tools
- Subagent maps persist across tool group lifecycles so late-arriving
  child tools still find their parent
- StrataThink-based XAML template with HeaderExtra agent badge
- Structured JSON subagent payloads via Utf8JsonWriter
- Subagent metadata helpers in ToolDisplayHelper
- Unit tests for parallel subagent grouping and payload refresh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: strengthen agent memory instructions to enforce continuous usage

The previous instructions were too passive — agents treated them as
optional. Rewrote AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md to be explicit that:
- Memory is not optional, it is core to the workflow
- Recall happens at session start, always, before any code
- Memories are stored as they happen, not batched at the end
- Concrete examples of what to store (including mundane context)
- The goal is a living knowledge base for future blank-context sessions

Also added an 'Agent instructions' section to README.md explaining
why pasting AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md into the system prompt is required,
not optional.

Add explicit type annotations across 25 files to resolve all remaining
TS7006 (parameter implicitly has an 'any' type) errors. Uses proper
types from existing imports where possible (RxChangeEventBulk,
MessageFromRemote, MessageToRemote, EventBulk, RxStorageChangeEvent,
PeerWithResponse, WebSocket, etc.) and falls back to 'any' only when
the proper type cannot be determined from context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve type annotation issues causing build failures

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
